dominants.dominant_desc:
The "Flicker-Vacuum" Combo
This is your most lethal trick in the mid-to-late game for picking off priority targets.
Activate Skill 1 (Shadow Stampede): Use the speed boost to run through the enemy backline.
Mark the Carry: Touch the enemy Marksman or Mage to mark them.
The Snap-Back: Just as the skill is about to end (or tap it again to end early), Flicker backward toward your tower or team.
The Result: The enemy will be dragged a massive distance into your team's range, and you can immediately follow up with Skill 2 for high bounce damage.

emblems.emblem_desc:
This is actually the preferred choice for high-rank players because Faramis is fundamentally a "utility" hero.
Movement Speed (Agility): Faramis has no blink skills. His Skill 1 gives him speed, but it has a cooldown. Having high base movement speed ensures you can position yourself for the perfect Ultimate without getting caught.
Cooldown Reduction: The Support emblem offers the highest CDR base stats. Faramis is only "OP" when his Ultimate is ready. Minimizing that downtime is priority #1.
Pull Yourself Together: This is the game-changer. It reduces the cooldown of your Flicker. Since the "Flicker-Pull" is Faramis's most dangerous engage tool, having it available more often means more successful ganks and picks.

equips.equip_desc:
Survive the Initial Dive: You have to stay alive long enough to press your Ultimate.
Spam the Ultimate: You need to have your "second life" realm ready for every single objective (Turtle/Lord).
Utility over Damage: Your Skill 2 is used to apply anti-heal and slow enemies rather than one-shotting them.
Key Roam Equipment Breakdown
1. Enchanted Talisman (The "Engine")
Why it's key: This is non-negotiable. Faramis is mana-hungry, but more importantly, he needs the 20% Cooldown Reduction (CDR) and the Magic Mastery passive, which increases the max CDR cap.
The Result: It allows you to use Skill 1 (Shadow state) more often to scout and escape, keeping you on the map longer.
2. Fleeting Time (The "Ultimate Reset")
Why it's key: This is the most important item for a Roamer. Its passive reduces the current cooldown of your Ultimate by 30% whenever you get a kill or an assist.
The Result: In a teamfight, if your team gets just 2 kills while your Ultimate is on cooldown, it will almost instantly be ready again. This allows you to "defend" your team twice in a long, drawn-out objective dance.
3. Flask of the Oasis (The "Shield Booster")
Why it's key: A newer staple for 2026 supports. When you heal or shield an ally (including the Spectre health from your Ultimate), if their HP is low, it triggers an extra massive shield.
The Result: It makes your Ultimate's "second life" even harder to burn through, effectively giving your teammates a third layer of health.
4. Dominance Ice (The "Aura of Weakness")
Why it's key: As a Roamer, you are often close to the enemy. Dominance Ice reduces the Shields, Healing, and Attack Speed of all nearby enemies.
The Result: It shuts down enemy lifesteal heroes (like Alucard or Ruby) and Marksmen just by you standing near them in your Shadow state.

hero.hero_overview:
Faramis is a Mid-Lane specialist (though he can flex into Roam) who thrives in the thick of teamfights. His kit revolves around gathering "Soul Fragments" to scale his power and reduce his death timer, and using his Ultimate to give his entire team a "second life."
Core Skills
Passive (Vicious Retrieval): Every 4s, hitting enemies with skills produces Soul Fragments. Absorbing these heals you, increases your Magic Power (up to 40 stacks), and permanently reduces your respawn timer (up to 90%).
Skill 1 (Shadow Stampede): Enter a shadow state to gain 70% movement speed and move through walls. Touching enemies marks them; when the state ends, you pull all marked enemies toward you.
Skill 2 (Ghost Bursters): Your main poke. It deals AoE damage that bounces between nearby enemies. The more enemies grouped together, the more it bounces and the harder it hits.
Ultimate (Cult Altar): Faramis creates a Nether Realm around him. Allies inside gain an extra "Spectre" health bar. If that bar is depleted, they enter a brief resurrection state where they can keep fighting.
hero.hero_strength:
Anti-Burst Protection: His Ultimate is the ultimate "NO" button to burst assassins like Saber, Gusion, or Eudora.
Incredible Sustain: Between his passive healing and the respawn timer reduction, Faramis is rarely off the map for long.
Terrain Mastery: His S1 allows him to ignore walls, making him an excellent roamer and escape artist.
High Teamfight Damage: His S2 bounces can melt groups of enemies, especially if they are pulled together by his S1.
hero.hero_weakness:
Short Range: Unlike long-range mages like Pharsa or Novaria, Faramis has to get close to the enemy to land his CC and damage.
Vulnerable to "Kiting": Highly mobile heroes who stay at a distance can poke him down before he can get in range.
Ultimate Timing: If you cast your Ultimate too early, the enemy can simply wait it out. If you cast it too late, your team is already dead.
Reliance on Stacks: To reach his full potential (and 10-second respawn timers), he needs to consistently collect souls throughout the game.

data.recommend: Faramis isn't just a support; he's a psychological weapon. If you follow this guide, you aren't just playing a hero—you're playing an insurance policy for your team and a nightmare for your enemies. I

data.title: Master of the Nether Realm

cooperates.cooperate_desc: These two are the best combo you could ever imagine because these two rely on how many enemies you're dealing your damage with. Use the ultimate of Carmilla first to link the enemies and follow up with Faramis first and second skill to burst down the enemies because Faramis based its damage on how many the enemies and Carmilla link those damage to the entire enemy team.

counters.counter_desc: Since Faramis' second skill was based on how many the targets where, he can somehow counter Gloo because Gloo's second skill produced many Goos which count as an enemy unit which Faramis can use to multiply his damage.

dominants.dominant_desc: When I use Faramis, I often use him as a setter and help my teammates to avoid being burst out in team fights. The flicker is a great choice for Faramis because you can surprise your enemies using your first skill and flicker and pull them near to your teammates. You can also use Faramis to zone during objectives using you first skill so that you can pull the enemies away from the objectives. Faramis can also disrupt enemies skills like Pharsa. For his ultimate, when your teammates are having a hard time in team fights, use it so that they won't get burst, you can also use it in advance when you know that the enemies will apply crowd control on your team because expect that when there is a crowd control, there's a damage following that controls.

emblems.emblem_desc: For the emblem talents, I use agility to have extra movement speed and rotate faster, I also use bargain hunter to buy items faster and lethal ignition for continuous damage and to also secure kills.
emblems.emblem_title: Extra movement speed, fast itemization and continues damage.

equips.equip_desc: This is the items that I use so that I can spam the first and second skill and to deal a huge amount of damage.

hero.hero_strength: Faramis is one of the heroes that can deal a massive damage during team fights and can be an actual setter of the team if you timed your first skill perfectly and pull many enemy heroes. With that, Faramis strength really depends on how many the enemies, that's why he can also clear lanes faster compared to other mages. Another thing about Faramis is he just don't deal massive damage, he can help the whole team avoid burst attacks with his ultimate, which makes him a very reliable hero during team fights.
hero.hero_weakness: The weaknesses and disadvantages I encountered when I play Faramis are his ultimate and his capability to fight against high hp heroes. About the ultimate, yes, you can save your teammates when they are low in hp but if the temporary hp breaks, there is a short immobilization to your team which enemies can use to kill your teammates, that's why you need to perfectly timed your ultimate before their low health so that they will have time to escape once the temporary hp breaks. Another thing is his capability against high hp heroes, when Faramis was cornered by a high hp hero he was having a hard time to fight back, especially when there's an enemy backup, that's why you need to stick with your teammates.

spell.spell_desc: This can be use to escape, surprise pull your enemies and to rescue your teammates with your ultimate faster.

counters.counter_desc: Bruno's ultimate counters Faramis, burst damage and his sustain with Haas' Claws make it difficult for Faramis to keep up with the pace.

dominants.dominant_desc: It is advised for faramis to aid exp lane so that both players will be able to share their exp and gold gain, getting the exp crab might help Faramis get to level 4 easily.

Faramis needs vision so that he will be able to poke enemies at ease without having to worry about ambushed. Quantum charge, his first skill, and the emblem setup synergizes so well that it's almost effective to gain stacks easily. This is when he needs to rack up stacks whenever possible.

emblems.emblem_desc: Faramis benefits from the support emblem, which provides a good amount of movement speed and cooldown reduction. Since this Faramis loadout is focused on getting items, bargain hunter is the best choice to get thunder belt as fast as he can. Tenacity is also a good choice for tier 2 emblem talent.

equips.equip_desc: Demon boots provide a good amount of mana regeneration for early to mid game, which can be later on replaced with any boots depending on your team composition needs. The queen's  wings and blood wings combo is effective in disengaging in team fights as it gives movement speed and reduction of current cooldown on skills on cooldown, allowing Faramis to have a chance at escaping with his first skill.

hero.hero_overview: Faramis has always been a good hero ever since utility jungle meta. His relevance however, was reduced due to assassin meta which has always been his weakness. With the new Thunder Belt rework, he can stand a chance surviving assassin attacks.
hero.hero_strength: Faramis getting his desired items as seen from this guide will be the beginning of his power spike. Going against immobile heroes or anyone he can outrange makes it easy for Faramis to increase his Thunder Belt stacks.
hero.hero_weakness: His early game squishiness makes him less irrelevant in the current meta, which is the assassin meta. Especially with the support emblem, he doesn't have much damage for we only focus on this loadout is his utility.

data.recommend: This could be Faramis' new core item, tested several times on both classic and ranked games.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker has always been a good spell for Faramis for he can be able to perform rescues with his ulti+flicker combo, while Revitalize is a good alternative to this.

equips.equip_desc: Prioritize Speed, Antiheal, then Cooldown which are the first three items. Andthen you are free to adjust the remaining three. Personally, I go for Lightning Truncheon for more AOE damage and then Wishing Lantern for enhanced damaged to tanks, and Divine Glaive as last itme to pierce through enemy defences.

hero.hero_overview: Faramis is not an easy hero to master. He is great in dealing damage and or poking multiple enemies but lags behind during 1 vs 1 fight. He always needs his team to finish enemies. Let's not forget that his ultimate is the most important thing about him as it can turn the tides of the battle. Make sure to time his ultimate at the most crucial time to give your team the upper advantage.
hero.hero_strength:
Laning: 

Faramis is good at wave clearing just make sure to use his 2nd skill covering all three minions when clearing them. Using his first skill, rush to the enemies duing laning phase and poke them with his second skill to gain stacks, securing the upperhand during the laning phase. After clearing the waves in the mid lane do not forget tovisit other lanes especially the exp lane just before the first turtle fight.
hero.hero_weakness:
Things to lookout for:

Faramis is not a good pick against AOE burst heroes as his ultimate can be easily countered by them. Beware of heroes such as Claude and Vexana.

Make sure to save your allies and not just use your ultimate for yourself bruh. 

If the enemies has so much Crowd Control skills, make sure to consider using the Purify spell to counter them. It is super crucial against Atlas(or setter tanks) as you can use Purify then Faramis' Ultimate just after Atlas uses his ultimate skill.

data.title: Faramis burst poke dmg

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Faramis does massive splash damage. If Carmilla lands a good ultimate. The passive from carmillas ult and Faramis splash damage can wipe enemy teams.
While I is is very vulnerable in her ult without flicker. Faramis can protect and zone enemies while ixia is in ult.
If tigreal sets multiple enemies Faramis can first skill to add even more CC then 2nd skill for massive splash damage.

counters.counter_desc: This can go honestly either way. While Valentina can copy Faramis ult. This would be dependent on how both teams play in team fights and who Faramis ults first.

Bane is a good counter to Faramis. He needs to get close with his first skill to pull enemies. If timed bane can ult then burst Faramis easily. Bane is a great counter to Faramis ult as well. Banes ult can almost wipe Faramis ult entirely with how much damage it does.
With ixia ult it can burn Faramis ult quite fast.

Faramis is a direct counter to gloo. When Gloo is in ultimate. One 2nd skill deals massive damage. Since Faramis is reliant on splash damage, Gloo ultimate brings more small units. This causes the splash damage to be greatly increased

dominants.dominant_desc: Faramis is slightly weak early. He needs to clear first wave after jg leash. Faramis then needs to gank either gold or exp. If you cannot secure a kill you will most likely burn enemies spell. In this early game stack your passive, poke enemies, support team. The minion right before turtle spawns. Tell your roam to clear the lastmid wave before turtIe then go to exp lane. If done correctly your roam will be level four. This cause you and exp to be level four as well.

Once mid game is reached. Faramis should have his first 3 core items. Depending on build his CD is a lot lower. He does really good splash damage and is actually considered a damage dealer instead of full utility. As long as you are rotating with your roam and jg to give ganks to exp or gold lane. You will excel at playing Faramis.

At this phase of the game Faramis should be full build. You do massage amounts of splash damage in team fights. You can setup plays, support team with your ultimate. Utilize poking enemies with your 2nd skill. Make sure to time ultimates. You do not want to ultimate early, this can cause enemies to make plays. Faramis damage is reliant on multiple units. This can be team fights or even a solo hero with creeps around. This boost Faramis splash damage to the max.

emblems.emblem_desc: For first emblem is inspire for extra 5% cd reduction or rupture if the enemy is tanky. For 2nd emblem I use bargain hunter or wilderness blessing. This helps with either getting Faramis core items faster or emblem to speed Faramis rotations for ganking. I use lethal ignition for the final emblem. Faramis is a great splash damage mage. Lethal ignition is great for ensuring kills.

equips.equip_desc: This is a full CD/DMG build. Faramis needs the first 3 core items. He heavily relies on cooldown reduction. Depending on enemy team you would buy glowing wand first if the enemy has a lot of sustain/heal.
A very similar build to Faramis most used build. With the change of wishing lantern. This item is a great item in teamfights with a tankier enemy team.

This Faramis is one used against a really squishy team. This build late game can one shot heros like MM, Mage, Jungle in team fights. Since Faramis does massive splash damage. Just one 2nd skill in a late game team fight can do more than half HP to multiple heros. The only downfall is mana will be an issue since Faramis loves to spam his 2nd skill. This build also cause all his skills to have slightly higher CD.

hero.hero_overview: Magic mage/support hero. A perfect hero who does burst splash damage while being able to support heavily in team fights. A hero who is utility/damage making him a great mage.
hero.hero_strength: Stacking gold from assist and farming to get core items in early game. Faramis excels mid to late game. Once core items are bought he can deal massive splash damage in team fights. Along with his crowd control pull and of course his ultimate in team fights. People see Faramis as a utility hero, but once you are full build late game. He does huge damage in team fights.
hero.hero_weakness: Faramis is slightly weak early game without core items. Although he still is great while using his ultimate and poking enemies with his 2nd skill. His cooldowns are quite long until mid-late game. Also if your team does fall behind quite far. Faramis will scale slow. Early game Faramis is completely utility hero. Once you reach mid-late Faramis does enough damage where you can make plays. He is not a mage that can 1v1 very well. His main source of damage is splash damage.

spell.spell_desc: Most matches flicker is a great spell. You can utilize Faramis first skill with a flicker to pull enemies towards your team. This can even catch enemies running away. Most people do not expect it. This also gives plays on flicker ultimate on to teammates who are close to dying but out of range. This is a great combo that surprises enemies. The 2nd spell would be purify. If a team had great CC heros purify is needed to be able to ultimate. Both of theses spells are dependent on enemy team comp

data.title: Top Global Faramis Mythic Immortal Rank

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
The Ube strat.
Works best when you have a tank jungle and sustain exp laner

counters.counter_desc: Can easily burst gloo specially when gloo is in using his ult

dominants.dominant_desc:
Use your second skill to poke the enemy and clear the minions fast. 
Visit lanes to gunk and to get stacks.
The more you stack the higher your damage and faster respawn timer
dominants.dominant_title: In early game focus on getting stacks

emblems.emblem_desc: Damage boost and mobility

equips.equip_desc: For a high AOE burst damage
A faster cool down and a high damage output

spell.spell_desc: For escape and fast response to clash
